name: PF6.Charts.Area

aliases.name: Series
aliases.comment: A named series of `(x, y)` data points for a multi-series area chart.
aliases.type: { label : String.String, data : List.List ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) }

values.name: fromData
values.comment:
Create a single-series area chart from a list of `(x, y)` pairs.

The x-axis is treated as a continuous numeric scale (use index 0, 1, 2... for
evenly-spaced time steps, or real timestamps as epoch seconds).

    chart =
        Area.fromData
            [ ( 0, 42 ), ( 1, 55 ), ( 2, 48 ), ( 3, 72 ), ( 4, 63 ) ]

values.name: fromSeries
toSvg
values.comment: Render the chart to an `Html msg` node.

values.name: withColor
values.comment:
Override the color for a single-series chart with a hex string. Default: PF6 primary blue.

Has no effect on multi-series charts (use `withTheme` to customise series colors).

values.name: withFillOpacity
values.comment: Set the fill opacity under each area line. Default: 0.20.

values.name: withHeight
values.comment: Set chart height in pixels. Default: 250.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`, setting axis, grid, label, and series colors.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title rendered above the chart.

values.name: withTooltips
values.comment:
Enable SVG `<title>` tooltips on each data point. Default: `False`.

When enabled, hovering over a data point shows a browser-native tooltip with
the x and y values (e.g. `"x: 3, y: 72"`). No ports or state required.

values.name: withWidth
values.comment: Set chart width in pixels. Default: 500.

values.name: withXLabel
values.comment: Set the x-axis label.

values.name: withYLabel
values.comment: Set the y-axis label.

name: PF6.Charts.Bar

unions.name: Orientation
unions.comment:
Bar orientation — vertical (default) or horizontal.

Use `Horizontal` when category labels are long or when you want to show
nominal data with more space for labeling (categories on the y-axis).

aliases.name: SeriesData
aliases.comment: Multi-series data: each series has a label and a value per category.

values.name: fromData
values.comment:
Create a bar chart from category labels and one or more value series.

For a single series, pass a list with one `SeriesData`.

    chart =
        Bar.fromData
            [ "Memory", "CPU", "Disk" ]
            [ { label = "Used", values = [ 70, 45, 82 ] } ]

For grouped bars:

    chart =
        Bar.fromData
            [ "Node 1", "Node 2", "Node 3" ]
            [ { label = "Used (GiB)", values = [ 8, 12, 6 ] }
            , { label = "Cached (GiB)", values = [ 3, 4, 2 ] }
            ]

values.name: toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withColors
values.comment: Override the color list for bars (one color per series).

values.name: withGrouped
values.comment:
When `True` (default), multiple series render as side-by-side grouped
bars. When `False`, series are stacked (use `Stack` for stacked area).

values.name: withHeight
values.comment: Set chart height in pixels. Default: 300.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withOrientation
values.comment:
Set the bar orientation. Default: `Vertical` (categories on x-axis).

Use `Horizontal` when category labels are long or for nominal variables —
bars extend left-to-right, categories are listed on the y-axis.

    Bar.fromData categories series
        |> Bar.withOrientation Bar.Horizontal
        |> Bar.toSvg

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title.

values.name: withTooltips
values.comment:
Enable SVG `<title>` tooltips on each bar. Default: `False`.

When enabled, hovering over a bar shows a browser-native tooltip with the
category and value (e.g. `"Memory · Used: 70"`). No ports or state required.

values.name: withWidth
values.comment: Set chart width in pixels. Default: 500.

values.name: withXLabel
values.comment: Set the x-axis label.

values.name: withYLabel
values.comment: Set the y-axis label.

aliases.type: { label : String.String, min : Basics.Float, q1 : Basics.Float, median : Basics.Float, q3 : Basics.Float, max : Basics.Float }

name: PF6.Charts.Bullet

values.name: fromData
values.comment:
Create a bullet chart.

Arguments: actual value, target value, maximum value (axis extent).

    chart =
        Bullet.fromData 62 75 100

This shows an actual value of 62, a target of 75, on a 0–100 scale.

values.name: toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withHeight
values.comment: Set chart height in pixels. Default: 120.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title (rendered left of the bar).

values.name: withUnit
values.comment: Set the unit label appended to axis tick values.

values.name: withWidth
values.comment: Set chart width in pixels. Default: 450.

name: PF6.Charts.Colors

values.name: axisLine
values.comment: Axis line and tick color — `#c7c7c7`.

values.name: background
values.comment: Default chart background — `#ffffff`.

values.name: blue100
values.comment: Blue 100 — `#92c5f9`. Lightest, used for tertiary fills.

values.name: blue200
values.comment: Blue 200 — `#4394e5`. Secondary series.

values.name: blue300
values.comment: Blue 300 — `#0066cc`. Primary series / active states.

values.name: blue400
values.comment: Blue 400 — `#004d99`. Dark series.

values.name: blue500
values.comment: Blue 500 — `#003366`. Darkest series.

values.name: blueFamily
values.comment:
Blue color family — five shades of PF6 blue from primary outward.

Use this when you want a monochromatic, on-brand palette (e.g. a grouped bar
chart where each series is a different blue shade). Ordered from most to least
prominent so the first series draws the eye.

    Bar.fromData categories values
        |> Bar.withColors Colors.blueFamily

values.name: green100
values.comment: Green 100 — `#bde5b8`.

values.name: gridLine
values.comment: Grid line color — `#c7c7c7`.

values.name: labelText
values.comment: Tick label and legend text color — `#383838`.

values.name: multiOrdered
values.comment:
Multi-ordered color scale for categorical data — cycles through blue,
green, teal, gold, orange for maximum categorical contrast.

    Bar.fromData categories values
        |> Bar.withColors Colors.multiOrdered

values.name: multiUnordered
values.comment:
Multi-unordered color scale for time-series data — keeps series visually
distinct across a full time axis (area charts, multi-line charts).

values.name: orange100
values.comment: Orange 100 — `#f4b678`.

values.name: orange200
values.comment: Orange 200 — `#ef9234`.

values.name: orange300
values.comment: Orange 300 — `#ec7a08`.

values.name: primary
values.comment: PF6 primary blue — `#0066cc`.

values.name: white
values.comment: White — `#ffffff`.

name: PF6.Charts.Donut

aliases.name: Slice
aliases.comment: A single slice: label and numeric value.

values.name: fromData
toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withCenterText
values.comment:
Set the text displayed in the hollow center of the donut.

The first argument is the primary metric (e.g. `"42"`), the second is a
sub-label (e.g. `"Running"`).

    Donut.fromData slices
        |> Donut.withCenterText "42" "Running"

values.name: withColors
values.comment: Override the slice color list.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withSize
values.comment: Set the overall SVG size (width = height). Default: 230.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title rendered below the donut.

values.name: withTooltips
values.comment:
Enable SVG `<title>` tooltips on each slice. Default: `False`.

When enabled, hovering over a slice shows a browser-native tooltip with the
label, value, and percentage (e.g. `"Running: 42 (76%)"`). No ports required.

name: PF6.Charts.DonutUtilization

unions.name: DonutUtilization
unions.comment: An opaque donut utilization chart configuration.

values.name: fromData
values.comment:
Create a donut utilization chart.

    chart =
        DonutUtilization.fromData 48 100

This shows 48 out of 100 (48%) utilization.

values.name: toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withDangerThreshold
values.comment:
Set the danger threshold percentage. Default: 90.

Above this percentage, the arc turns red/orange.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withSize
values.comment: Set the overall SVG size. Default: 230.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title rendered below the donut.

values.name: withWarningThreshold
values.comment:
Set the warning threshold percentage. Default: 75.

Above this percentage, the arc turns gold/yellow.

name: PF6.Charts.Scatter

unions.name: Overlay
unions.comment:
Optional overlay drawn on top of scatter points.

  - `NoOverlay` — points only (default).
  - `WithLine` — connects each series' points with a line (sorted by x).
  - `WithArea` — fills the region under each series' points.

aliases.name: Series
aliases.comment: A named series of `(x, y)` scatter points.
aliases.type: { label : String.String, data : List.List ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) }

values.name: fromData
values.comment: Create a single-series scatter chart from `(x, y)` pairs.

values.name: fromSeries
values.comment: Create a scatter chart from multiple named series.

values.name: toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withHeight
values.comment: Set chart height in pixels. Default: 300.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withOverlay
values.comment:
Add a line or area overlay connecting each series' points. Default: `NoOverlay`.

    -- Scatter + connecting line (sorted by x):
    Scatter.fromSeries series
        |> Scatter.withOverlay Scatter.WithLine
        |> Scatter.toSvg

    -- Scatter + filled area:
    Scatter.fromSeries series
        |> Scatter.withOverlay Scatter.WithArea
        |> Scatter.toSvg

values.name: withPointRadius
values.comment: Set the radius of scatter points in pixels. Default: 5.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title.

values.name: withTooltips
values.comment:
Enable SVG `<title>` tooltips on each point. Default: `False`.

When enabled, hovering over a scatter point shows a browser-native tooltip
with the x and y coordinates (e.g. `"(3.5, 72.0)"`). No ports or state required.

values.name: withWidth
values.comment: Set chart width in pixels. Default: 500.

values.name: withXLabel
values.comment: Set the x-axis label.

values.name: withYLabel
values.comment: Set the y-axis label.

name: PF6.Charts.Skeleton
comment:
Loading skeleton placeholders for every PF6.Charts chart type.

Use these in place of a real chart while data is loading. They match the
chart's dimensions and show a shimmer animation consistent with PatternFly's
skeleton component.

    -- In your view:
    if model.loading then
        Skeleton.area 560 250

    else
        Area.fromData model.data
            |> Area.withWidth 560
            |> Area.toSvg


# Cartesian skeletons

@docs area, line, bar, scatter, stack, threshold, bullet, boxPlot


# Circular skeletons

@docs donut, donutUtilization, pie


# Inline skeletons

@docs sparkline

name: PF6.Charts.Theme
comment:
Chart theme system for PF6 — light and dark modes.

Mirrors the pattern from `PF6.Theme` in elm-ui-patternfly, adapted for
chart-specific tokens (axis, grid, series colors).


# Types

@docs Theme, Mode


# Constructors

@docs light, dark, fromMode


# Bridging from a UI theme library

`PF6.Charts` intentionally has no dependency on `mdgriffith/elm-ui` or any
specific UI theme library. If you are already using `lenards/elm-ui-patternfly`
(or a future `elm-patternfly` built on `elm/html`), use `UiThemeColors` and
`fromUiThemeColors` to bridge your theme into chart tokens.

@docs UiThemeColors, fromUiThemeColors

**Example — bridging from `lenards/elm-ui-patternfly`:**

```elm
import Element exposing (toRgb)
import PF6.Theme as UiTheme
import PF6.Charts.Theme as ChartTheme

{-| Convert an elm-ui Color to a 6-digit hex string.
    Requires rtfeldman/elm-hex or a similar hex-encoding package.
-}
colorToHex : Element.Color -> String
colorToHex color =
    let
        { red, green, blue } =
            Element.toRgb color

        byte n =
            String.padLeft 2 '0' (Hex.toString (round (n * 255)))
    in
    "#" ++ byte red ++ byte green ++ byte blue

{-| Derive chart tokens from a PF6 UI theme.
-}
chartTheme : UiTheme.Theme -> ChartTheme.Theme
chartTheme uiTheme =
    ChartTheme.fromUiThemeColors
        { primary = colorToHex (UiTheme.primary uiTheme)
        , labelText = colorToHex (UiTheme.text uiTheme)
        , background = colorToHex (UiTheme.backgroundDefault uiTheme)
        , borderDefault = colorToHex (UiTheme.borderDefault uiTheme)
        }
```

The same pattern will work for a future `elm-patternfly` library built on
`elm/html` — just swap the `colorToHex` helper for whatever color type that
library exposes.


# Accessors

@docs primaryColor, seriesColor, axisColor, gridColor, labelColor, backgroundColor, fontFamily

unions.name: Theme
unions.comment: An opaque type holding all color tokens for chart rendering.

aliases.name: UiThemeColors
aliases.comment:
A plain record of hex color strings extracted from a UI theme library.

Pass this to `fromUiThemeColors` to get a `Theme` without requiring any
dependency on `mdgriffith/elm-ui` or `lenards/elm-ui-patternfly`.

All fields are 6-digit hex strings, e.g. `"#0066cc"`.
aliases.type: { primary : String.String, labelText : String.String, background : String.String, borderDefault : String.String }

values.name: axisColor
values.comment: Axis line and tick mark color.

values.name: backgroundColor
values.comment: Chart background color.

values.name: dark
values.comment: PF6 dark mode chart theme. Uses lighter colors on dark backgrounds.

values.name: fontFamily
values.comment: Font family string for chart text (tick labels, legends, annotations).

values.name: fromMode
values.comment:
Derive a theme from a `Mode` value.

    fromMode Light == light
    fromMode Dark  == dark

values.name: fromUiThemeColors
values.comment:
Build a chart `Theme` from a `UiThemeColors` record.

The chart `series` color scale defaults to `Colors.multiOrdered` with `primary`
as the first color. Axis and grid lines use `borderDefault`. Label text uses
`labelText`. See the module doc for a full bridging example.

values.name: gridColor
values.comment: Grid line color.

values.name: labelColor
values.comment: Tick label and legend text color.

values.name: light
values.comment: PF6 light mode chart theme.

values.name: primaryColor
values.comment: The primary series color for single-series charts.

values.name: seriesColor
values.comment:
All series colors as an ordered list. Use index into this list to color
each series in multi-series charts. Falls back to primary if index is out of range.

name: PF6.Charts.Threshold

values.name: fromData
values.comment:
Create a threshold chart from `(x, y)` data pairs.

Add thresholds using `withThresholdLabel`.

    chart =
        Threshold.fromData cpuData
            |> Threshold.withThresholdLabel 80 "#f0ab00" "Warning"
            |> Threshold.withThresholdLabel 95 "#c9190b" "Critical"

values.name: toSvg
values.comment: Render to `Html msg`.

values.name: withColor
values.comment: Override the main series color.

values.name: withHeight
values.comment: Set chart height in pixels. Default: 250.

values.name: withLoading
values.comment: Show a skeleton placeholder instead of the chart while data is loading.

values.name: withTheme
values.comment: Apply a `Theme`.

values.name: withThresholdLabel
values.comment:
Add a horizontal threshold line.

    chart
        |> Threshold.withThresholdLabel 80 "#f0ab00" "Warning (80%)"

values.name: withTitle
values.comment: Set the chart title.

values.name: withWidth
values.comment: Set chart width in pixels. Default: 500.

values.name: withXLabel
values.comment: Set the x-axis label.

values.name: withYLabel
values.comment: Set the y-axis label.
